zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John FitzHugh II of Rippingale and Kirkby Underwood於1235年出生在英國英格蘭時，他的父親Sir John FitzHugh I of Rippingale and Kirkby Underwood40歲，母親Ivette37歲。他和Annabel至少有1個兒子。他去世於1262年在他的家鄉享年27歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John FitzHugh II of Rippingale and Kirkby Underwood于1235年出生在英国英格兰时，他的父亲Sir John FitzHugh I of Rippingale and Kirkby Underwood40岁，母亲Ivette37岁。他和Annabel至少有1个儿子。他去世于1262年在他的家乡享年27岁。

nameMeanings.name: FitzHugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Northamptonshire): from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Hugh (see Hugh ), ‘son of Hugh’.

History: William Fitzhugh (1651–1701), from Bedford, England, emigrated to VA c. 1670 and established himself on the Potomac River in what was then Stafford County, VA, as a planter and exporter. He also practiced law, was a member of the Virginia House of Burgesses, and served in 1687 as lieutenant colonel of the county militia.

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
Altered form of English   Fitch  .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Japanese: written 井伊 ‘well’ and ‘that’, the name one of Japan's great daimyō families, originally based in Tōtōmi (now part of Shizuoka prefecture), later in Kōzuke (now Gunma prefecture) and Ōmi (now Shiga prefecture). Other variants of the name substitute either character with similar sounding characters such as 飯 ‘cooked rice’, 居 ‘residence’, and so forth. It was formerly Romanized as Iyi. — Note: In the population figure published by the US Census Bureau, the Roman number II (meaning ‘the second’, i.e. ‘the younger’ of the two bearers of the name) is apparently also counted as a surname Ii.

History: Ii Naomasa (1561–1602) was awarded lands in Kōzuke and Ōmi for his service in the civil wars that won the shōgunate (military dictatorship) for Tokugawa Ieyasu. His family built the beautiful castle of Hikone, which still stands. His descendant Ii Naosuke (1815–1860) ruled Japan for two years as Tairō (Great Elder) and played a major part in signing treaties that opened Japan to foreign trade at the end of the Tokugawa Shōgunate. He was assassinated by adherents of the anti-foreign party.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Rippingale
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from Rippingale (Lincs).

nameMeanings.name: and
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of French Canadian Hénault or directly of French Hunault (see Henault 2).
Catalan (Añó): Castilianized form of Anyó, probably from the old personal name Annone.
Japanese: variant of   Anno  .

variants.name: Anno
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Masayuki, Tadashi, Yoshito.
Japanese: written 安野 ‘peaceful plain’. This surname is rare in Japan proper, and may have originated in the Ryūkyū Islands, where it is pronounced   Ano  .

variants.name: Henault
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Andre, Emile, Lucien, Martial, Pierre, Camille, Fernand, Laurier, Monique, Normand, Philippe.
French (Hénault): from the ancient Germanic personal name Haginwald, composed of the elements hagin ‘enclosure, protected place’ and wald ‘power, authority’.
Altered form (Hénault) of French Hunault: from the ancient Germanic personal name Hunwald, composed of the elements hun ‘bear cub’ and wald ‘power, authority’. Compare Ano and Eno .

nameMeanings.name: Kirkby
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Kirby  .

variants.name: Kirby
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places in northern and eastern England called Kirby or Kirkby, from Old Norse kirkja ‘church’ + bȳ ‘farmstead, village’.
Irish: adopted for Ó Garmhaic ‘descendant of Cíarmhac’, a personal name meaning ‘dark son’. Compare Kerwick .
Irish: Anglicized form of Mac Geirble ‘son of Geirble’, a personal name of uncertain origin. The name is preserved in the townland name of Carrowkeribly in County Mayo.

nameMeanings.name: Underwood
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived below a wood on a hillside, from Middle English under + wude, wode ‘wood’, or a habitational name for someone from any place so named.

History: Joseph Underwood emigrated from England to Watertown, MA, in 1637. William Underwood came from England to Concord, MA, before 1640, later settling in Chelmsford, MA. In VA, the name was established by Thomas William Underwood, who came from Norfolk, England, in 1675.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John FitzHugh II of Rippingale and Kirkby Underwood was born in 1235, in England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir John FitzHugh I of Rippingale and Kirkby Underwood, was 40 and his mother, Ivette, was 37. He had at least 1 son with Annabel. He died in 1262, in his hometown, at the age of 27.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John FitzHugh II of Rippingale and Kirkby Underwood est né en 1235, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir John FitzHugh I of Rippingale and Kirkby Underwood, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Ivette, avait 37 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Annabel. Il est décédé en 1262, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 27 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John FitzHugh II of Rippingale and Kirkby Underwood 1235 in England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John FitzHugh I of Rippingale and Kirkby Underwood 40 und seine Mutter Ivette 37. Er hatte mit Annabel mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1262 im Alter von 27 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John FitzHugh II of Rippingale and Kirkby Underwood è nato il nel 1235 nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Sir John FitzHugh I of Rippingale and Kirkby Underwood, aveva 40 anni e il suo madre, Ivette, aveva 37 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Annabel. Morì nel 1262, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 27 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John FitzHugh II of Rippingale and Kirkby Underwoodが1235年にイギリスイギリスで生まれた時，父Sir John FitzHugh I of Rippingale and Kirkby Underwoodは40歳，母Ivetteは37歳であった。彼はAnnabelとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1262年に故郷で27歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John FitzHugh II of Rippingale and Kirkby Underwood(이)가 1235년 영국 잉글랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John FitzHugh I of Rippingale and Kirkby Underwood(은)는 40세였고, 그의 어머니 Ivette(은)는 37세였다. 그는 Annabel(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1262년 그의 고향에서 27세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John FitzHugh II of Rippingale and Kirkby Underwood nasceu em 1235, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir John FitzHugh I of Rippingale and Kirkby Underwood, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Ivette, tinha 37 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Annabel. Ele faleceu em 1262, em sua cidade natal, com 27 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John FitzHugh II of Rippingale and Kirkby Underwood родился в 1235 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir John FitzHugh I of Rippingale and Kirkby Underwood было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Ivette, 37. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Annabel. Он умер в 1262 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 27 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir John FitzHugh II of Rippingale and Kirkby Underwood nació en 1235, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir John FitzHugh I of Rippingale and Kirkby Underwood, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Ivette, tenía 37 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Annabel. Murió en 1262, en su pueblo, a la edad de 27 años.
